title: T-105 — Location Tree screen (SET-014 §12.2)
prompt:
# T-105 — Location Tree screen (SET-014 §12.2)

PRD: 02-SETTINGS-PRD.md §12.2 (Infrastructure CRUD UI table)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Build the Location Tree screen from §12.2 (drag-drop deferred to Phase 2; CSV import in scope). Renders ltree-backed `locations` hierarchy as expandable tree, per-warehouse sub-trees, and a CSV import action that posts to T-029 location server actions. V-SET-60 (level=parent.level+1) enforced server-side; UI surfaces errors inline.

## Implementation contract
1. Page at apps/web/app/(admin)/settings/infra/locations/page.tsx renders Server Component fetching `locations` rows joined by warehouse, ordered by `path`.
2. Tree primitive (shadcn Accordion + custom indent) shows level 1 (warehouse root) -> 2 (zone) -> 3 (rack) -> 4 (bin), expandable per node.
3. Toolbar: warehouse filter dropdown + 'Import CSV' button opening a CSV upload dialog (file input -> POST to /api/infra/locations/import dispatcher that calls T-029 internally).
4. Drag-and-drop reorder is OUT OF SCOPE (Phase 2 per §12.2) — render rows in tree order only.
5. RBAC: requires `settings.infra.read` for view, `settings.infra.update` for CSV import.

## Files
- apps/web/app/(admin)/settings/infra/locations/page.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/(admin)/settings/infra/locations/page.test.tsx [create]

## Acceptance criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` array.

## Test strategy
1. RED: RTL test mounting tree with seed of 1 warehouse + 2 zones + 4 bins; assert ordered render, CSV upload triggers Server Action with file blob.
   `pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run apps/web/app/(admin)/settings/infra/locations/page.test.tsx`
2. RED must fail.
3. GREEN: implement Server Component + tree + CSV upload dialog.
4. Re-run RED, then `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not implement drag-drop (Phase 2 per §12.2).
- Do not derive ltree paths client-side; rely on T-029 server-side materialization.
- Do not import @radix-ui/* outside packages/ui (§0).

Stack: TS + Next.js App Router + Drizzle + pnpm + vitest + Playwright + Server Actions. PRD: 02-SETTINGS-PRD.md v3.5.

pipeline_inputs.description: Location Tree screen (SET-014): hierarchical view + CSV import; drag-drop deferred to Phase 2.
pipeline_inputs.details: PRD §12.2 lists SET-014 (Location Tree) with capability `drag-drop (Phase 2), import CSV`. Tree must reflect ltree path order; CSV import dispatches to T-029 createLocation per row, which validates V-SET-60 (level=parent.level+1) server-side and surfaces row-level errors aggregated in a toast.
out_of_scope: Per-row Location Edit modal (separate SET-015 screen)
acceptance_criteria: Given the locations tree renders, when 1 warehouse + 2 zones + 4 bins exist, then the tree shows the warehouse root, expandable to 2 zones, each expandable to its bins in `path` order.
Given a CSV with 5 rows where row 3 has `level=3` but `parent.level=1`, when imported, then T-029 returns LEVEL_GAP for row 3 and the UI shows a row-level error toast referencing V-SET-60.
Given the warehouse filter is set to a single warehouse, when the tree re-renders, then only that warehouse's locations are visible.
Given the caller lacks `settings.infra.update`, when they open the page, then the Import CSV button is disabled with an aria-label explaining insufficient permissions.
test_strategy: RED: RTL test renders tree with seed; asserts ordered indent + filter + CSV import error path.
GREEN: implement tree + CSV upload dispatching to T-029.
risk_red_lines: Do not derive ltree paths client-side; rely on T-029 server-side materialization.
